_description: Zeal Exercise Database Schema & Sample Exercises. Every exercise in the app MUST follow this exact structure. No field is optional. The generation engine depends on every field — missing data causes rules to silently fail.

_enum_definitions._75_hard_note: 75 Hard is NOT a workout style — it is a meta-layer / program mode that generates two daily sessions by pulling from the styles above. It does not appear in eligible_styles. See Master Rules Document Section 4 for details.

_field_reference.id: string — Unique identifier. Use snake_case. Example: 'barbell_back_squat'
_field_reference.name: string — Display name shown in the app. Example: 'Barbell Back Squat'
_field_reference.aliases: string[] — Alternate search names. Example: ['back squat', 'bb squat']
_field_reference.movement_pattern: enum — Primary movement classification. One value from movement_pattern enum.
_field_reference.difficulty_tier: enum — Skill/complexity level. Affects which fitness levels can access it.
_field_reference.eligible_styles: string[] — Which workout styles may include this exercise. Values: strength, bodybuilding, crossfit, hyrox, mobility, hiit, cardio, pilates. Note: 75 Hard is a program mode, not a style — it pulls from these styles.
_field_reference.primary_muscles: string[] — Main muscles trained. Used for split filtering and volume tracking.
_field_reference.secondary_muscles: string[] — Assisting muscles. NOT used for split filtering but counted for volume awareness.
_field_reference.joints_loaded: string[] — Joints bearing significant load. Used for injury/contraindication filtering.
_field_reference.equipment_required: string[] — ALL equipment needed. Exercise only eligible if EVERY item is in user's inventory. Use ['bodyweight'] for no-equipment exercises.
_field_reference.equipment_optional: string[] — Helpful but not required accessories.
_field_reference.is_unilateral: boolean — If true, reps display as 'ea' and set_duration doubles in time calculations.
_field_reference.is_compound: boolean — Multi-joint movement? Affects rest period tier and exercise ordering in some styles.
_field_reference.default_tempo_seconds_per_rep: number — Average time for one full rep at controlled pace. For time-based exercises (planks, carries, cardio intervals), this is the duration in seconds per 'rep'. CRITICAL for time accuracy engine.
_field_reference.setup_time_seconds: number — Time to get into position / set up equipment. Added to transition buffer.
_field_reference.contraindication_tags: string[] — Life cases / injuries where this exercise is BLOCKED. Hard safety filter.
_field_reference.rom_requirement: enum — Range of motion needed. Helps filter for ROM-limited users (pregnancy, injury).
_field_reference.spinal_load: enum — How much the spine is loaded. Key filter for pregnancy and back injuries.
_field_reference.position: enum — Body position during execution. Supine filtered for pregnancy after week 20.
_field_reference.weight_increment: number — Standard progression jump in lbs. Barbell = 5, dumbbell = 2.5, cable = 5, bodyweight = 0 (progress via reps). Cardio exercises = 0 (progress via duration/speed).
_field_reference.rep_range_floor: number — Minimum productive reps. Going below this means the weight is too heavy. For cardio, this is minimum intervals/rounds.
_field_reference.rep_range_ceiling: number — Max reps before the user should increase weight. For cardio, max intervals/rounds.
_field_reference.default_load_table: object — Starting weight suggestions keyed by gender_fitnesslevel. Each has a 'multiplier' (× bodyweight) and 'absolute_fallback_lbs' for cases where bodyweight math produces odd numbers. Bodyweight / cardio / pilates exercises use 0 for all values.
_field_reference.is_warmup_eligible: boolean — Can this serve as a warmup movement?
_field_reference.is_cooldown_eligible: boolean — Can this serve as a cooldown/mobility movement?
_field_reference.warmup_for_muscles: string[] — If warmup-eligible, which muscles does it prepare?
_field_reference.cooldown_for_muscles: string[] — If cooldown-eligible, which muscles does it help recover?
_field_reference.substitutes: string[] — Ranked exercise IDs: same muscle/pattern, different equipment/difficulty. Best substitute first.
_field_reference.variation_family: string — Groups related variations. Shared novelty penalty in rotation logic.
_field_reference.style_popularity: object — Per-style popularity scores from -100 to 100. Keys are eligible style names, values are numeric. Higher = more likely to be selected for that style. Only includes styles from this exercise's eligible_styles. Example: { strength: 90, bodybuilding: 70, crossfit: 40 }
_field_reference.tracking_metric: object — How the exercise is measured. { primary: enum, alternates: enum[] }. Primary is the default metric (reps, distance_meters, calories, time_seconds, max_weight). Alternates are valid alternative metrics (empty for most exercises).
_field_reference.execution_logic: enum — How reps are distributed for the movement. 'bilateral' = both sides simultaneously (squat, bench). 'alternating' = alternates sides each rep (walking lunges). 'per_side' = all reps one side then switch (single-arm rows). Must be 'bilateral' when is_unilateral is false.
_field_reference.default_rest_sec: number — Baseline rest period in seconds for this exercise. Heavy compounds 120-180, moderate compounds 90, isolation 60, core 45, cardio 60, mobility/pilates 20. Style rules may override but this is the exercise-specific default.
_field_reference.standard_rx_weight: object | null — Competition prescribed weights. { male_rx_lbs, female_rx_lbs, male_scaled_lbs, female_scaled_lbs, male_pro_lbs, female_pro_lbs }. null for exercises without competitive standards. Used for CrossFit Rx and Hyrox race standards.
_field_reference.media_url: string — URL to hosted video/GIF/image for exercise demonstration. Empty string as placeholder until media hosting is configured.
_field_reference.equipment_note: Equipment IDs bosu_ball, pilates_ring, and stability_ball are defined in the enum for future use but currently have no exercises requiring or optionally using them.

exercises.name: Chest Press Machine
exercises.notes: Machine-guided pressing. Good beginner substitute for barbell bench.

exercises.name: Cable Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Cable variation of lat pulldown. Constant tension throughout ROM.

exercises.name: Machine Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Machine-guided lateral raise. Easier to isolate side delts.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Band lateral raise. Increasing tension at the top of the movement.

exercises.name: Trap Bar Carry
exercises.notes: Carry with trap bar. Easier grip than dumbbells for heavy loads.

exercises.name: Cable Front Raise
exercises.notes: Cable front raise. Constant tension through the entire ROM.

exercises.name: Plate Front Raise
exercises.notes: Hold a weight plate by the edges for front raises.

exercises.name: Cable Rear Delt Fly
exercises.notes: Cable rear delt fly with cables at eye height. Constant tension.

exercises.name: Barbell Shrug
exercises.notes: Barbell shrug. Allows heavier loads than dumbbells.

exercises.name: Cable Shrug
exercises.notes: Cable shrug. Constant tension throughout ROM.

exercises.name: Hang Snatch
exercises.notes: Snatch from hang position (above knees). Less technical than full snatch from floor.

exercises.name: Towel Pull-Up
exercises.notes: Pull-up gripping towels draped over the bar. Extreme grip demand.

exercises.name: DB Hang Clean & Jerk
aliases: single arm db clean and jerk
